chat.author: Christopher Locke
chat.text: Just a note to say the Internet Society Foundation does provide research grants for the kind of research Kurtis just described - go to our website for the Research Grants details

chat.author: Christopher Locke
chat.text: This years application window closed but it runs annually

chat.author: Mohit Tahiliani
chat.text:
APNIC provides funds for similar activities in the Asia Pacific region:
https://apnic.foundation/grants/isif-asia/
(closed for this year)

chat.author: Dave Plonka
chat.text: tnx, Christoper, I captured this in the notes. Feel free to revise if called for: https://notes.ietf.org/notes-ietf-126-gaia

chat.author: Kurtis Heimerl
chat.text: @christoper some of our research is ISOC funded! It's been great, we've submitted a followup as well

chat.author: Dave Plonka
chat.text: Tnx, Mohit. I will capture this similarly in the notes.

chat.author: Dan York
chat.text: FYI, this page about OFDS at the Hackathon has links to all the tools that Steve Song is talking about: https://github.com/Open-Telecoms-Data/ofds-ietf-hackathon/

chat.author: Dan York
chat.text:
I recently started helping Steve with OFDS work... and I had no idea that there was such a HUGE open source ecosystem around this QGIS software: https://qgis.org/ 
There are all sorts of tutorials and info.

chat.author: Kurtis Heimerl
chat.text: is this functionally an oss arcgis?

chat.author: Sarah Tarrant
chat.text: Seems like sharing this data should be more innately regular. The secrecy is disappointingly suspicious.
